run_metadata.notes: Performance & cost sweep based on repository snapshot from 2026-03-06.

agent.name: performance-cost-auditor
agent.role: Identifies performance regressions, redundant calls, and cost risks in the codebase.

findings.title: Unoptimized SELECT * Queries
findings.description: Multiple Supabase queries use .select('*') to fetch all columns. This wastes bandwidth and memory, especially for tables with large text or JSONB fields like projects and task_prompts.

proof_hooks.summary: Projects adapter fetches all columns with select('*').
Auth adapter returns all columns with select('*').
Credentials adapter returns every column via select('*').

findings.impact: Fetching unbounded columns increases payload sizes and slows Supabase responses, raising bandwidth cost for large rows.

suggested_fix.approach: Specify only required columns in Supabase select calls for each query.

findings.title: Missing staleTime in React Query Hooks
findings.description: Almost all project hooks (useProjects, useProjectTasks, etc.) omit the staleTime configuration. With the default of 0ms, this triggers redundant network requests on every window focus, component remount, and network reconnect.

proof_hooks.summary: Project hooks omit staleTime so queries refetch on focus.

findings.impact: Redundant fetches create unnecessary Supabase calls and slower UX on navigation.

suggested_fix.approach: Provide a sensible staleTime (for example 1-5 minutes) for project data in useQuery options.

findings.title: Missing Code Splitting for Large Libraries
findings.description: The application relies on heavy dependencies (monaco-editor, @xyflow/react, html2canvas, jspdf, recharts) but lacks any React.lazy() or dynamic import() code-splitting boundaries. This forces all users to download the full weight of these libraries upfront, severely impacting initial load performance.

proof_hooks.summary: App.tsx imports heavy components without lazy boundaries.
Vite config does not define route-level dynamic imports for heavy vendors.

findings.impact: All users download monaco-editor, xyflow, recharts, and export libraries upfront, hurting initial load and increasing bandwidth costs.

suggested_fix.approach: Use React.lazy and Suspense for editor/flow routes and configure manualChunks or dynamic imports to split heavy vendors.
tests_needed: Run bundle analyzer to verify vendor chunks are split

next_actions.action: Replace select('*') with column-specific queries in Supabase adapters.
next_actions.rationale: Reduces response size, speeds queries, and aligns with least-data-needed guidance.

next_actions.action: Set staleTime defaults for project query hooks.
next_actions.rationale: Prevents redundant refetches on focus/remount and cuts unnecessary Supabase calls.

next_actions.action: Add lazy boundaries and route-level chunking for heavy UI surfaces.
next_actions.rationale: Shrinks initial bundle size so users avoid downloading editor/flow vendors upfront.
